name: pure_lego_hopf_contact_symplectic_bridge
classification_note: Standalone pure-geometry lego for the Hopf-contact-symplectic bridge identity. Establishes that ker(α) on S³ is compatible with the symplectic area form on S² under Hopf projection: ω_S²(dπ(h₁),dπ(h₂)) = −2·dα(h₁,h₂). Distinct from sim_torch_hopf_connection.py (Berry phase/connection) and sim_hopf_pointwise_pullback.py (density matrix pullback). Uses pytorch (load_bearing) + z3 UNSAT + sympy polynomial identity.

pytorch.reason: Primary engine: Hopf map, Jacobian (dπ), horizontal basis via α-projection, omega_s2 area form, pullback ratio verification at 100 random S³ points, Reeb verticality J·R=0, pushforward surjectivity check, antipodal cover

pyg.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

z3.reason: UNSAT proof: assuming Reeb field is NOT vertical (J·R ≠ 0) at any point contradicts the algebraic identity derived from the Jacobian and Reeb definitions. Structural impossibility of non-verticality.

cvc5.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

sympy.reason: Symbolic verification that J·R = 0 as a polynomial identity in (x₀,x₁,x₂,x₃), and that the pullback ratio is exactly −2 at the reference point p=(1,0,0,0) via symbolic matrix computation.

clifford.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

geomstats.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

e3nn.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

rustworkx.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

xgi.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

toponetx.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

gudhi.reason: not relevant to Hopf bridge probe

P2_pushforward_nonzero.description: dπ(h) ≠ 0 for h ∈ ker(α) at 50 random S³ points

P4_ground_truth_north_pole.description: Explicit verify at p=(1,0,0,0): lhs=−4, rhs=2, ratio=−2

P6_pushforward_rank2.description: dπ|_{T_pS³} has rank 2 everywhere (ker = span{R}, image = T_{π(p)}S²)

N1_vertical_annihilated.description: Reeb field R maps to zero under dπ (vertical = annihilated)

N2_wrong_coupling_rejected.description: c=−1 is wrong: residual |lhs − (−1)·rhs| is large

N3_z3_reeb_verticality_unsat.claim: J·R[0] ≠ 0 on unit S³ is UNSAT (algebraic identity forces J·R=0)
N3_z3_reeb_verticality_unsat.description: z3 UNSAT proof: non-verticality of Reeb under dπ is structurally impossible

N4_sympy_JR_zero_polynomial.description: Sympy confirms J·R = 0 as polynomial identity (all three rows)

B1_near_pole.description: Bridge identity holds at near-pole point (x₀≈1)

B2_equatorial_fiber.description: Bridge identity holds at equatorial fiber point (x₀=x₁=0)

B3_antipodal_cover.description: π(p) = π(−p): antipodal S³ pair covers same S² point (double cover)

B4_large_batch_stability.description: Bridge identity stable across 100 random points, no numerical blowup

summary.reeb_vertical: J * R = 0 algebraically and confirmed by z3 UNSAT
